findings.summary: 18 capacitors with non-standard 'U' reference prefix misclassified as 'ic'; Crystal load caps (U8, U9 = 30pF each) not detected because they are misclassified as ICs; Transistor circuit Q1 gate_dri...

correct.description: power_regulators correctly identifies U14 as TPS5430DDA with topology=switching, inductor=L1, output_rail=+3.3V, has_bootstrap=true.

bus_analysis.uart correctly finds two UART channel pairs: SIM_RX/SIM_TX connecting STM32F30 and U16 (SIM808), and USART2_RX/TX on the STM32. SPI=[] is also correct since the SPI1 signals terminate at header connector J2.

incorrect.description: Components U1,U2,U3,U4,U5,U6,U8,U9,U11,U12,U13,U15,U17,U18,U19,U20,U21,U22 are all Device:C_Small symbols with capacitor values (100n, 10n, 22p, etc.) but are classified as type 'ic' because the classifier uses the reference prefix letter. Statistics report 22 ICs and 1 capacitor; actual breakdown should be ~4 ICs and 19 capacitors. Real ICs in the design: STM32F30 (STM32F030C8Tx), U14 (TPS5430DDA), U16 (SIM808), U23 (SMF05CT1G).

crystal_circuits reports U7 (8MHz crystal) with load_caps=[]. U8 and U9 (both 30pF Device:C_Small) are the crystal load caps but since they carry 'U' reference prefixes, they are typed as 'ic' and the crystal circuit detector cannot find them. A cascading failure from the misclassification bug.

The P-channel MOSFET Q1 (AO3407) transistor_circuits entry incorrectly lists U17, U19, U20, etc. (all capacitors) as gate_driver_ics, and SIM808 (U16) appears 15 times. This is a direct cascade of the U-prefix misclassification: the gate node net contains many components that are all marked 'ic', leading to absurd gate_driver_ics population. The gate_net is reported as 'GND' which appears wrong for a switching MOSFET.

signal_analysis.decoupling_analysis shows only C1 (220uF tantalum) on +3.3V with cap_count=1. All the ceramic bypass caps (U2,U3,U4,U5,U6,U12 = 100nF each; U1,U17 = 4.7uF; U18 = 1uF; etc.) are invisible to decoupling analysis because they are classified as 'ic' instead of 'capacitor'.

findings.summary: ESD protection for D- reported as has_esd_protection=False despite U1 (USBLC6-2SC6) protecting it; I2C pullups R2 and R3 (10k to +3V3) not detected; has_pull_up=False for both SDA and SCL; MIC5504-...

correct.description: power_regulators correctly identifies U2 as LDO topology with output_rail=+3V3, estimated_vout=3.3, vref_source=fixed_suffix from '3.3YM5' part number suffix.

rf_matching correctly identifies J18 (SMA) as the antenna with L_match topology using L2 (27nH) and C7 (47pF), targeting U3 (NEO-M8P). RC filter and LC filter for the RF path are also correctly reported at 338MHz cutoff / 141MHz resonance.

transistor_circuits correctly identifies Q1 as a BJT in LED driver configuration: emitter grounded, D6 (GREEN LED) in collector path, base resistor R14 (220R), current resistor R16 (220R) on +3V3 power rail.

incorrect.description: design_observations shows D- with has_esd_protection=False and D+ with has_esd_protection=True, yet protection_devices correctly lists U1's protected_nets as [D+, D-, DM, DP]. The asymmetry between D+ and D- detection is a bug in how the observation cross-references the protection device's protected_nets list. DP and DM are similarly False despite being listed.

The NEO-M8P (U3) I2C pins connect through 33R series resistors (R8, R9) to the SDA{slash}#CS and SCL{slash}CLK label nets, where R2 and R3 (10k) provide pullups to +3V3. The bus analyzer only checks for pullups on the net directly connected to the IC pin (__unnamed_4, __unnamed_5), missing the pullups one net hop away through the series resistors. This is a real network topology limitation that produces a false 'missing pullup' conclusion.
